issues.description: The proposed INV20 replacement conjunct (\A a \in OBArchivers : \A e \in ob_archiver_store[a] : e.session >= ob_primary[e.market]) is NOT an invariant. After OBViewChange(m) fires, ob_primary[m] increments to S+1 while msgs_ob_broadcast[m] still contains old-session S messages (OBViewChange has UNCHANGED msgs_ob_broadcast at line 452). ArchiverIngestOB(a) can then ingest an old-session message, creating a record with e.session = S < ob_primary[m] = S+1 in ob_archiver_store[a]. The proposed invariant is immediately violated. The GC action cannot prevent this because both GC and ingest are enabled simultaneously after view change (ob_alive[m] = TRUE for both). TLC would produce a counterexample: Init -> OBProduceEffect -> ArchiverIngestOB [session S record in store] -> OBCrash -> OBViewChange [ob_primary advances to S+1] -> invariant already violated even WITHOUT another ingest, because the session S record ingested BEFORE the view change is still present and now has e.session < ob_primary[e.market].
issues.spec_lines: 452 (UNCHANGED msgs_ob_broadcast in OBViewChange), 652-658 (ArchiverIngestOB ingests any session), 1007-1016 (INV20 original)

issues.description: The GC action removes ALL effects where e.session < ob_primary[m] for a given market. This includes effects ingested BEFORE the view change that may still be needed. Consider: OB session S produces effects seq 1..5. Archivers ingest all 5. OBCrash, then OBViewChange recovers up to seq 3 using OBArchiverSeqsForSession(m, S). View change completes, ob_primary advances to S+1. Now the GC fires and removes ALL session S effects from archivers. If another OBCrash + OBViewChange occurs immediately (back-to-back), the recovery for session S+1 reads OBArchiverSeqsForSession(m, S+1) which has nothing (new session only started, no effects produced yet). This is fine for session S+1 recovery. BUT the old session S effects are permanently lost from archivers. The DA guarantee for OB effects relies on archivers retaining data. Unlike CE archivers which have checkpoint-based GC (effects below checkpoint are on L1), OB has no external persistence layer mentioned in the trust model. Removing old-session OB archiver data without an equivalent checkpoint or L1 anchoring mechanism means those effects are unrecoverable. The proposal does not address whether OB effects have an alternative persistence path.
issues.spec_lines: 997-1006 (INV20 DA monotonicity comment mentioning OB archivers never GC'd)

issues.description: The proposed UNCHANGED clause lists obBackupVars but the original spec's variable groupings need verification. The action modifies ob_archiver_store (within archiverVars tuple). The UNCHANGED lists individual archiver vars (ce_archiver_store, ce_archiver_ack_count, ob_archiver_pending, oms_archiver_store) but not ob_archiver_store (correctly, since it's modified). However, it also claims UNCHANGED obBackupVars. This is correct since ob_backup_applied is the only obBackupVars component and is not touched. No actual error here, but the listing of individual archiver vars instead of using the archiverVars tuple minus ob_archiver_store is consistent with ArchiverGCByAcks/ArchiverGCByCheckpoint patterns (lines 701-704, 712-715). Downgraded from issue to note.

strengths: Correct identification of the CE/OB archiver GC asymmetry (CE has two GC paths, OB has zero)
Correct ob_alive guard preventing GC during view change (mutual exclusion with OBViewChange's ~ob_alive guard)
Correct identification that ob_archiver_pending is a separate concern from ob_archiver_store
Session-scoped filtering is the right granularity for OB archiver GC if it were to be added
Correct that OMSViewChange reads current ob_primary[m] and does not need old-session archiver data
recommendation: The critical issue (INV20 replacement is not an invariant) is fatal. The proposed invariant fails the moment any OB archiver record exists from a session before the current ob_primary, which happens routinely (effects ingested during session S persist across view change to session S+1). Two possible fixes: (1) Remove the INV20 conjunct entirely and keep the GC action with only the original INV20 (CE-only). The OB archiver GC would then be 'unguarded' by INV20 but the original comment about OB effects never being removed would need updating to 'OB effects only removed for old sessions'. (2) Reformulate the invariant as a conditional: effects can only be removed AFTER the session has advanced, which is what the GC guard already enforces. But this is tautological (restating the guard as an invariant). The deeper question (medium severity issue) is whether removing old-session OB archiver data is safe from a DA perspective at all, given no OB checkpoint/L1 anchor mechanism exists. The original spec intentionally keeps OB archiver data forever. The asymmetry may be deliberate, not a bug. Recommend rejecting and asking the proposer to either (a) justify OB DA safety without old-session archiver data by citing an external persistence mechanism, or (b) introduce an OB checkpoint mechanism analogous to CE's before enabling GC.
